message:
test(rch): update existing probe test fixtures for new error_code field

Mechanical follow-up: the `WorkerProbeResult` struct gained the
`error_code: Option<String>` field in this branch. Two existing test
modules constructed literal `WorkerProbeResult { ... }` values and
therefore stopped compiling until they pass either
`error_code: None` or `error_code: Some(...)` explicitly.

rch/src/commands/mod.rs:
- `probe_result_healthy_json_shape` adds `error_code: None` to the
  literal and asserts the JSON has no `error_code` field (the
  `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` derive
  elides it, matching the existing `error` field's behaviour).
- `probe_result_unreachable_json_shape` adds
  `error_code: Some("RCH-E108")` (network connection refused) and
  asserts the JSON carries `error_code == "RCH-E108"` — pinning
  the string-form serialization for downstream consumers.

rch/src/ui/probe.rs:
- Three `WorkerProbeResult` literals in the UI render test get
  matching updates: two healthy probes get `error_code: None`, and
  the "Connection refused" fixture gets
  `error_code: Some("RCH-E108")`. The UI assertion that prints
  the error also now implicitly exercises the code path that
  reads `error_code` when non-None.

No production behaviour change; the structs were already populated
correctly in runtime code by the earlier `ssh_error_code` wiring.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -598,6 +598,7 @@ mod tests {
                     status: "ok".to_string(),
                     latency_ms: Some(25),
                     error: None,
+                    error_code: None,
                 },
                 ssh_ok: true,
                 rsync_available: Some(true),
@@ -612,6 +613,7 @@ mod tests {
                     status: "ok".to_string(),
                     latency_ms: Some(120),
                     error: None,
+                    error_code: None,
                 },
                 ssh_ok: true,
                 rsync_available: Some(true),
@@ -626,6 +628,7 @@ mod tests {
                     status: "failed".to_string(),
                     latency_ms: None,
                     error: Some("Connection refused".to_string()),
+                    error_code: Some("RCH-E108".to_string()),
                 },
                 ssh_ok: false,
                 rsync_available: None,
@@ -688,6 +691,7 @@ mod tests {
                 status: "ok".to_string(),
                 latency_ms: Some(100),
                 error: None,
+                error_code: None,
             },
             ssh_ok: true,
             rsync_available: None,
@@ -707,6 +711,7 @@ mod tests {
                 status: "ok".to_string(),
                 latency_ms: Some(300),
                 error: None,
+                error_code: None,
             },
             ssh_ok: true,
             rsync_available: None,
@@ -726,6 +731,7 @@ mod tests {
                 status: "failed".to_string(),
                 latency_ms: None,
                 error: Some("Error".to_string()),
+                error_code: None,
             },
             ssh_ok: false,
             rsync_available: None,
